project: OpenTabs Platform — Browser Resource Inspection Tools
description: Add browser tools for listing and reading all resources (JS, CSS, HTML, images, etc.) loaded by a page, using the Chrome DevTools Protocol Page domain. This enables AI agents to inspect a webapp's source code — especially JavaScript files — to understand its API layer, data models, authentication patterns, and internal architecture when creating OpenTabs plugins.

userStories.title: Add browser_list_resources tool to enumerate all page resources
userStories.description: As an AI agent reverse-engineering a webapp, I want to list all resources (scripts, stylesheets, documents, images, fonts, etc.) loaded by a page so that I can identify which JavaScript files contain API client code, which CSS files define the UI, and what the overall resource structure looks like.
acceptanceCriteria: New file platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/list-resources.ts defines the browser_list_resources tool using defineBrowserTool
Tool accepts tabId (required) and optional type filter (string, e.g. 'Script', 'Stylesheet', 'Document', 'Image', 'Font', 'Other')
Tool dispatches to extension via 'browser.listResources' method
Extension handler in browser-commands.ts implements handleBrowserListResources
Handler uses CDP: if network capture is active for the tab (isCapturing(tabId)), reuses the existing debugger session; otherwise attaches debugger, does work, then detaches
Handler calls chrome.debugger.sendCommand({tabId}, 'Page.enable') then chrome.debugger.sendCommand({tabId}, 'Page.getResourceTree') to get all resources
Returns { frames: [{ url, securityOrigin }], resources: [{ url, type, mimeType, contentLength }] } — flattened across all frames
When type filter is provided, only resources matching that type are included in the response
Resources are sorted by type then URL for easy scanning
'browser.listResources' added to dispatch table in message-router.ts
'browser.listResources' added to ALLOWED_METHODS in offscreen/index.ts
handleBrowserListResources imported and wired in message-router.ts
Tool registered in platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/index.ts
Tool description explains it lists all resources loaded by the page (from browser cache, not re-fetched) and suggests using type filter 'Script' to find JavaScript files for API analysis

If debugger attachment fails because another debugger (e.g., DevTools) is attached and network capture is not running, returns a clear error message suggesting the user close DevTools or enable network capture first
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
bun run test:e2e passes

userStories.title: Add browser_get_resource_content tool to read a specific resource
userStories.description: As an AI agent inspecting a webapp's JavaScript source code, I want to read the content of a specific resource by URL so that I can analyze API client code, understand data models, find authentication patterns, and discover API endpoints in the source.
acceptanceCriteria: New file platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/get-resource-content.ts defines the browser_get_resource_content tool
Tool accepts tabId (required), url (required — the resource URL to fetch), and maxLength (optional, defaults to 500000)
Tool dispatches to extension via 'browser.getResourceContent' method
Extension handler in browser-commands.ts implements handleBrowserGetResourceContent
Handler uses same debugger management pattern as US-001 — reuses debugger if network capture is active, otherwise attach/detach
Handler calls chrome.debugger.sendCommand({tabId}, 'Page.enable') then chrome.debugger.sendCommand({tabId}, 'Page.getResourceContent', { frameId, url })
The frameId is obtained by first calling Page.getResourceTree and finding which frame owns the requested URL
Returns { url, content, base64Encoded, mimeType, truncated } — content is the resource text (or base64 for binary)
Text content exceeding maxLength is truncated with '... (truncated)' suffix and truncated=true
For base64-encoded text resources (mimeType starts with 'text/' or is 'application/javascript', 'application/json', 'application/xml'), decode to UTF-8 string before returning
For binary resources (images, fonts, wasm), return base64-encoded content as-is with base64Encoded=true
'browser.getResourceContent' added to dispatch table in message-router.ts
'browser.getResourceContent' added to ALLOWED_METHODS in offscreen/index.ts
handleBrowserGetResourceContent imported and wired in message-router.ts
Tool registered in platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/index.ts

Tool description explains it reads resource content from browser cache (no re-fetch) and suggests using browser_list_resources first to find resource URLs, and mentions its usefulness for reading minified JS to understand API patterns
If the resource URL is not found in the page's resource tree, returns a clear error message
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
bun run test:e2e passes
userStories.notes:
CDP Page.getResourceContent takes {frameId: string, url: string} and returns {content: string, base64Encoded: boolean}. The frameId is required — it's the frame that loaded the resource. To find it, first call Page.getResourceTree and walk the tree to find which frame contains the requested URL. Cache the tree briefly or call it fresh each time (fresh is simpler and more correct).

The tricky part is finding the right frameId. The resource tree is nested: { frame: {id, url}, childFrames: [{frame: {id, url}, childFrames: [...], resources: [...]}], resources: [{url, type, mimeType}] }. Walk recursively, checking each frame's resources array for a URL match. Return the frame.id of the frame that owns the resource.

For base64 decoding of text content: if base64Encoded is true and the mimeType looks like text, decode with `atob(content)`. Note: `atob()` handles ASCII; for UTF-8 you'd need TextDecoder. But most page resources are ASCII-compatible, so atob is sufficient. If decoding fails, return the base64 as-is.

Default maxLength is 500000 (500KB) — generous enough for large minified JS files but prevents memory issues. Large JS bundles (like webpack chunks) can be 1-5MB minified, so users may want to increase this.

Files to create/edit: (1) browser-commands.ts — add handleBrowserGetResourceContent, (2) message-router.ts — add import + dispatch entry, (3) offscreen/index.ts — add to ALLOWED_METHODS, (4) Create get-resource-content.ts in browser-tools/, (5) browser-tools/index.ts — add to tools array.

userStories.title: Add E2E tests for browser_list_resources and browser_get_resource_content
userStories.description: As a developer, I want comprehensive E2E tests for the resource tools so that the full pipeline (MCP client → MCP server → WebSocket → extension → CDP Page domain) is verified end-to-end.
acceptanceCriteria: New test describe block 'Browser tools — resource inspection' added to e2e/browser-tools.e2e.ts

Test: browser_list_resources returns resources for a loaded page — opens test server tab, lists resources, verifies at least one Script and one Document resource is returned with url, type, and mimeType fields
Test: browser_list_resources with type filter 'Script' returns only Script resources
Test: browser_list_resources returns error for non-existent tab
Test: browser_get_resource_content retrieves the content of a JavaScript resource — lists resources, picks a Script URL, fetches content, verifies non-empty string returned
Test: browser_get_resource_content returns error for non-existent URL
Test: browser_get_resource_content returns error for non-existent tab
Test: resource tools work while network capture is active (debugger sharing) — enables capture, lists resources, gets content, disables capture — no errors
BROWSER_TOOL_NAMES in helpers.ts updated to include 'browser_list_resources' and 'browser_get_resource_content'
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
bun run test:e2e passes
userStories.notes:
Follow the existing patterns in browser-tools.e2e.ts. Use openTestServerTab helper to create a loaded page. The test server serves JS (the test page has inline scripts or loads scripts) so browser_list_resources should find at least Document + Script resources.

For the debugger sharing test: enable network capture first (which attaches debugger), then call list_resources and get_resource_content (which should reuse the existing debugger session), then disable capture. This verifies the isCapturing() check in the handler works correctly.

Add the new tool names to BROWSER_TOOL_NAMES array in helpers.ts so the 'browser tools appear in tools/list' test covers them.
